name: Rapalogs Coverage Repair Assessment for June 11, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 11 backfill assessment found that the published Rapalogs outlook was activity-only and missing source, study, finding, review, registry, safety, and seminal-anchor support.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for a low-confidence human_functional_benefit Rapalogs public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages PEARL one-year human safety and healthspan results, the Harrison 2009 mammalian lifespan anchor, a 2025 vertebrate lifespan meta-analysis, a direct mTOR inhibitors aging review, active and completed no-results registry watch items, finding-level context for the already-public sirolimus older-adults registry, and support-map normalization for oral and topical rapamycin records. Coverage is not strong because durable human functional, disease, frailty, mortality, dose-schedule, and long-term preventive-safety evidence remain unresolved.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages PEARL as a result-bearing one-year randomized rapamycin trial, keeps older-adult mTOR/TORC1 vaccine and infection trials visible, normalizes the oral and topical rapamycin records, and adds no-results registry findings for sirolimus, REVERSE, Everolimus Aging, RESTOR, Rapa/cMRI, and RAP PAC.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The remaining issue is an evidence gap rather than a coverage gap: human records are mixed and still do not show durable broad aging benefit.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged public claim depends on interventional rapalog trials, registry watch, and review-level translation boundaries rather than observational or natural-experiment evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future safety work may still use off-label observational cohorts or pharmacoepidemiology, but that is not required to make the current low-confidence interventional support map adequate.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages the canonical Harrison 2009 ITP mouse lifespan source and a 2025 vertebrate lifespan meta-analysis as preclinical and review-level anchors.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal coverage is sufficient for current context, but it remains translation support rather than human longevity evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged mTOR inhibitors aging review, RESTOR, RAP PAC, existing TORC1 immune trial records, and immunosenescence/mTOR review context cover target engagement, mTORC1 selectivity, immune, metabolic, and tissue PK/PD boundaries.

The support map now foregrounds the RTB101 phase 3 miss, PEARL's null primary visceral-fat endpoint, the oral rapamycin pilot's lack of measured cognitive or physical-performance improvement, and no-results registry status for multiple direct trials.

PEARL and the oral rapamycin pilot improve short- and medium-term safety coverage, while RESTOR and RAP PAC stage dose, PK/PD, and toxicity watch context. Long-term preventive safety, immunosuppression, metabolic effects, wound-healing risk, mucosal effects, and durability remain thin.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Upgrade only after larger, longer, dose-specific preventive-use safety and durability evidence is available.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages no-results or posted-result context for PEARL, NCT05237687, REVERSE, Everolimus Aging, RESTOR, Rapa/cMRI, and RAP PAC, and records NCT06789900 and NCT07191353 as direct but bone/exercise boundary watch items.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue ordinary surveillance for posted results, linked publications, status changes, and direct bone/exercise rapalog trials.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages the 2025 vertebrate lifespan meta-analysis and the 2023 mTOR inhibitors aging review, while retaining the 2026 immunosenescence, vaccine, microbiota, and mTOR review as taxonomy context.
Harrison 2009 is staged as the canonical mammalian lifespan anchor, and the recent meta-analysis triangulates the broader vertebrate rapamycin lifespan literature.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage is adequate for current public claims; a stronger audit could later add additional ITP dose, sex, schedule, and healthspan anchors.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged outlook separates Rapalogs from immune rejuvenation, autophagy induction, senomorphics, dermatologic topical evidence, microbiome/vaccine review context, and fasting-mimicking-diet combination trials.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future publication and surveillance should keep branch-level support maps separate when new results arrive.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair directly checked ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for PEARL, sirolimus older adults, REVERSE, Everolimus Aging, RESTOR, Rapa/cMRI, RAP PAC, and two bone/exercise boundary trials; fetched PubMed metadata and abstracts for PEARL, Harrison 2009, the 2025 vertebrate lifespan meta-analysis, and the 2023 mTOR inhibitors aging review; and compared those sources against the published Rapalogs outlook, May 30 surveillance bundle, June 11 backfill assessment, and linked local immune-rejuvenation and senomorphic records.
source_selection_notes: PEARL was staged because it is a peer-reviewed randomized one-year rapamycin trial in healthy adults and directly repairs human safety and healthspan coverage.
Harrison 2009 and PMID 40532901 were staged to repair seminal and recent-review animal lifespan coverage.
PMID 37142830 was staged to repair review-level safety, translation, and taxonomy-boundary coverage for mTOR inhibitors.

NCT07354620, NCT05835999, NCT06658093, NCT04742777, and NCT05949658 were staged because they are direct rapalog registry watch items with aging, PK/PD, methylation, cardiac, metabolic, or safety endpoints and no posted results.
NCT06789900 and NCT07191353 were screened but not staged because they are direct rapalog bone/exercise boundary trials and would be better handled in later surveillance or skeletal-aging context.
This assessment does not publish public records; it records the internal coverage state of the staged repair bundle.

known_gaps.description: No staged source shows replicated durable human disease, frailty, mortality, or broad healthy-aging benefit from rapalog use.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue surveillance and only strengthen confidence if PEARL-like signals replicate or active rapalog trials post durable functional or clinical outcomes with acceptable safety.

known_gaps.description: Preventive long-term safety, immunosuppression, metabolic risk, mucosal effects, wound healing, dose scheduling, and durability remain unresolved for older adults.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep safety-limitations review mandatory before publication and track RESTOR, RAP PAC, Everolimus Aging, and longer PEARL follow-up or replication.

known_gaps.description: Several direct registries remain active, recruiting, or completed with no posted results, including NCT05237687, NCT07354620, NCT05835999, NCT06658093, NCT04742777, and NCT05949658.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Return to ordinary surveillance after review and publication, with explicit watch on posted results, linked publications, and status changes.

known_gaps.description: NCT06789900 and NCT07191353 are direct rapalog bone/exercise boundary trials but were not staged in this bounded repair to keep the core Rapalogs support map focused.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Reconsider these records in the next Rapalogs registry pass or in a skeletal-aging/bone-health evidence map.

next_coverage_action: Review the staged Rapalogs coverage-repair bundle through source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, taxonomy-mapping, and safety-limitations lanes. If approved and published, return Rapalogs to ordinary surveillance with explicit watch on active and completed no-results registries plus PEARL replication.
